summary: Ukrainian forces are deploying E-300 drones, rebuilt from light aircraft with combat payloads and extended range, in the Zaporizhzhia direction, following earlier use along the front line. Russian forces are actively countering Ukrainian drones, with the "Yolka" anti-drone system successfully downing fixed-wing "Hornet" strike drones, while Ukrainian FPV pilots are targeting Russian infantry, including a documented instance of a Russian soldier charging a drone with a bayonet and being destroyed. Ukrainian kamikaze drones struck the Afipsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai on March 14, 2026, causing a major fire. Russian operators of the "Rubikon" system reported 136 targets destroyed on March 14, including personnel and FPV strikes on Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs across multiple directions. Ukrainian FPV crews are also using drones to neutralize enemy infantry, including forcing surrender attempts before striking, and targeting artillery positions with fiber-optic drones, continuing the attrition of Russian tube artillery.
